core package

Submodules

core.cleaner_engine module

  1. Data Processing Module

core.cleaner_engine.backward_fill_strategy(series: Series) → Series

Backward fill NaN values.

core.cleaner_engine.clean_dataframe(df: DataFrame, handle_missing: bool = True, missing_strategy: str = 'mean', remove_duplicates: bool = True, duplicate_subset: List[str] = None) → DataFrame

Executes the main data cleaning pipeline using a functional approach.

This function acts as the primary orchestrator for the cleaning module, applying a series of specialized, smaller cleaning functions (like removing duplicates and handling missing values) to produce a finalized dataset.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – The original DataFrame to be cleaned.

  • handle_missing (bool, optional) – Whether to process and impute missing values. Defaults to True.

  • missing_strategy (str, optional) – The strategy to use for imputing numeric missing values (e.g., ‘mean’, ‘median’). Defaults to ‘mean’.

  • remove_duplicates (bool, optional) – Whether to drop duplicate rows. Defaults to True.

  • duplicate_subset (List[str], optional) – Specific column names to consider when identifying duplicates. If None, all columns are considered. Defaults to None.

Returns:

A new, cleaned copy of the DataFrame with the specified

operations applied.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

core.cleaner_engine.convert_to_numeric(df: DataFrame, columns: List[str]) → DataFrame

Required columns ko numeric type mein convert krke pura dataframe return Non-convertible ko NaN set

core.cleaner_engine.detect_missing_values(df: DataFrame) → Dict[str, float]

Missing values detect krke dictionary return Dictionary has column names and percentage of missing values

core.cleaner_engine.forward_fill_strategy(series: Series) → Series

Forward fill NaN values.

core.cleaner_engine.get_cleaning_summary(df_before: DataFrame, df_after: DataFrame) → Dict

Generates a summary of the data cleaning operations performed.

Parameters:

  • df_before (pd.DataFrame) – The original DataFrame before cleaning.

  • df_after (pd.DataFrame) – The resulting DataFrame after the cleaning process.

Returns:

A dictionary containing the summary statistics and changes made

during the cleaning process (e.g., rows removed, missing values handled).

Return type:

Dict

core.cleaner_engine.handle_missing_values_in_column(series: Series, strategy: str = 'mean') → Series

ek column ki missing values handle krke return

Parameters:

  • series – Series with potential missing values

  • strategy – Strategy to use (‘mean’, ‘median’, ‘mode’, ‘forward_fill’, ‘backward_fill’)

  • ;) (-> insb strategies k upr functions bnakr ek dict m define kiye hue - functional approach)

core.cleaner_engine.handle_missing_values_in_dataframe(df: DataFrame, numeric_strategy: str = 'mean', drop_all_missing: bool = True) → DataFrame

pury dataframe ki missing values handle krke return completely null rows ko drop krny ki option bhi hai

core.cleaner_engine.identify_numeric_columns(df: DataFrame) → List[str]

Numeric columns identify krke unki list return

core.cleaner_engine.mean_strategy(series: Series) → float

Calculate mean, ignoring NaN values.

core.cleaner_engine.median_strategy(series: Series) → float

Calculate median, ignoring NaN values.

core.cleaner_engine.mode_strategy(series: Series) → float

Calculate mode, ignoring NaN values.

core.cleaner_engine.remove_duplicate_rows(df: DataFrame, subset: List[str] = None) → DataFrame

Remove duplicate rows from DataFrame.

core.cleaner_engine.validate_data_types(df: DataFrame) → Dict[str, str]

Datatypes validate krke ek dictionary return dictionary mein column names aur unke datatypes honge

core.contracts module

class core.contracts.DataSink(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: Protocol

Outbound Abstraction: The Core calls this to save data. Any Output module must implement this method signature.

write(records: List[dict], config: dict = None) → None
class core.contracts.PipelineService(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: Protocol

Inbound Abstraction: The Input Module calls this to send data to the Core.

execute(raw_data: List[Any]) → None

Bases: PipelineService

execute(raw_data: List[Any], config_array) → None

core.melting_engine module

core.melting_engine.get_metadata_columns(df: DataFrame) → List[str]

Non year columns (metadata) extract krke ek list mein return

core.melting_engine.get_year_columns(df: DataFrame) → List[str]

saray year columns extract krke ek sorted list mein return

core.melting_engine.reshape_to_long_format(df: DataFrame) → DataFrame

Table ko reshape kia hai. Instead of a separate column for each year number, we have a single Year column, a Country column, and a GDP column. Usmein insb k combinations hein.
